displayTitle: Flow-Down: From Customer Spec to Your Bench

beats.narration: Two shops, same code, completely different programs. The code sets the floor — your employer's documents build the house.

payload.title: FLOW-DOWN
payload.sub: How customer requirements reach your inspection bench.

beats.narration: Customer requirements flow down: purchase order into the quality manual, manual into your VT procedure, and onto the bench as a traveler.
Trace one requirement: the spec demands critical examination, the procedure sets 1,000 lux, and your bench sheet repeats it — at the surface.
Every line on your checklist traces to a sentence up top. Changing one takes written authorization through the same chain — never a verbal okay.

chips.t: Every line traces to the top
A verbal okay is not a basis

beats.narration: So here's the rule: if it isn't written down the chain, it isn't authorized. Good intentions aren't authorization — controlled documents are.

payload.sub: Authorization travels the same written chain as the requirement.
